source: pred.gg public profile + own aggregate baselines

archetype.label: The Sparrow & Zinx Two-Trick
archetype.receipt: two heroes beat the field: Sparrow (62.8% over 106 games) and Zinx (57.6% over 126 games) — each worth extra wins vs the average player on them.

entries.change: Move your midlane games to carry
Queue carry instead of your average mix
entries.receipt: 53.5% there vs 49.9% overall, 188 games of evidence

ledger.note: wins per 100 games spent on each change; entries overlap and do not sum

plan: Queue carry as primary. It is your best role at 53.5% over 188 games (your overall is 49.9%). Set carry as secondary, not the other way around.
Two-hero rule for the climb: Sparrow and Zinx. Compared with the average player on the same hero, you win about 10.4 and 7.3 more games per 100 on them.
Your champion pool is 27+ heroes wide and your top three are only 22% of your games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on your top three.
Park Countess, Lt. Belica, Feng Mao, Mourn for ranked: 39% over 118; 42% over 95; 25% over 59; 33% over 33. Keep them for normals.
The math: you are at 595 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. Your all-time peak is 824.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward your own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where you win, not why

insights.title: Goldilocks324 brings out your best
insights.finding: Your record alongside Goldilocks324 is 56% over 193 ranked games — your strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of you queue, make it this one.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: 20 pentakills on record and a 26-kill spree. Cuban has receipts.

insights.title: Your lean, relative to the squad: vision work
insights.finding: Cuban's profile sits mid-pack on most axes; the clearest lean is vision work at 0.8x the squad average. Steady is a style too — the lineup can build around predictability.
